title: rubus rivularis subsp. lusaticus
intro: Rubus rivularis subsp. lusaticus, also known as the Lusatian raspberry or wild raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus genus of brambles. It is a type of edible raspberry that is native to Europe and is valued for its medicinal properties and health benefits. The plant's leaves and fruits have been used in traditional medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions, due to their high content of antioxidants and essential nutrients. The herbal preparations of Rubus rivularis subsp. lusaticus include infusions, tinctures, and jams, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
